nextSession.thesis: PRE-CLOSE (15:00 ET, cash session OPEN) — position INTO the 7/13 close and overnight into the marquee catalyst: June CPI + a HEAVY Q2 big-bank open (JPM/WFC/BAC/C/GS) BOTH land pre-open TOMORROW (Tue 7/14 8:30 ET / ~7am ET). The tape softened through the day (SPY 753→749.78 intraday, QQQ 718.33→712.7 and now UNDER its 50-day, VIX 16.44→16.95) — a mild de-risk into the print, NOT a break; all core still bull-stacked. Posture into the bell: HOLD the structural bull-stacks, HEDGE the overnight CPI gap while VIX (16.95) is still cheap, take the tool-aligned XLI→XLE rotation only on an RS-cross, and stay OUT of havens (GLD/SLV/BTC all Downtrend). Positive-gamma / orderly, so the calm is a positioning window, not an all-clear.

actions.subject: Overnight SPY/SPX downside into 7/14 CPI + bank open
actions.rationale: CPI and a five-name big-bank open BOTH hit pre-open tomorrow into a short-vol tape that drifted lower all afternoon (SPY −0.43% on the day, QQQ lost its 50-day, VIX +0.51 more to 16.95). VIX sub-17 keeps defined-risk downside cheap; the weekend-of-data gap is unhedged in the price. Buy the protection BEFORE the bell, not after the number.
actions.structuralAnchor: SPY 749.78 bull-stack (20>50>200), only −1.03% off the 52-week high (~757.6) with VIX 16.95 — spot near the highs + cheap vol = maximum asymmetry to hedge a known binary.
actions.trigger: Put protection / put-spread or a small VIX-call overlay on before the 7/13 close; size to the CPI gap, not a trend reversal.
actions.invalidation: A cool/in-line CPI + orderly bank open with SPY reclaiming 753 and VIX back under 16 — let the hedge decay; do not roll it into a standing short.

actions.subject: SPY / QQQ / SPMO / VGT core longs
actions.rationale: The core is still bull-stacked with strong medium-term momentum (SPY mom126/252 +9.32%/+21.14%; QQQ mom63 +16.76%, mom252 +28.93%). Today’s intraday fade is a pullback to support ahead of a binary, not a structural break — QQQ slipping fractionally under its 50-day (−0.41%) is a caution flag to WATCH, not a sell.
actions.trigger: Hold while SPY holds the rising 50-day (~741); let the core carry, do not add into the CPI print.
actions.invalidation: A confirmed daily close below the SPY 50-day (~741) or a broad loss of the QQQ/VGT/SPMO bull-stacks.

actions.subject: Trim rolling XLI / cooling XLK → add XLE
actions.rationale: The tool-aligned 2-week RRG accel says energy is the freshest rotation INTO: XLE Improving ↑ (accel +1.13, the board’s best) with rsMom6m +14.7 and +11.65% over its 200-day, while XLI is Weakening ↓ (accel −2.49, the sharpest roll on the board) and XLK is Weakening ↓/out. Softer crude fading the CPI headline tail keeps this an early, defensible cyclical rotation.
actions.structuralAnchor: XLE rsRatio 99.71 / rsMom 101.56 (Improving quadrant), tangled MA stack turning; XLI rsRatio 100.17 but rsMom 99.08 and accel −2.49 (rolling over).
actions.trigger: Add XLE on its RS-ratio crossing 100 (into the Leading quadrant); fund it by trimming XLI on any failure of its rising 50-day.
actions.invalidation: XLE acceleration rolls back negative or oil (USO, mom21 −12.4) breaks lower, aborting the energy turn.

actions.subject: Extended semis (SOXX) into weakening RRG + CPI vol
actions.rationale: SOXX is the strongest structure on the board (8/12 bull-stacked, +43.65% over its 200-day) but that is exactly the problem into a binary: Weakening ↓/out on the 2-week accel (rsMom1m −1.42) and stretched far above trend. Trim the extension ahead of CPI; keep the core — this is de-risking a stretched leader, not selling the theme.
actions.structuralAnchor: SOXX +43.65% over its 200-day, rsMom 99.08 (below 100 = Weakening); MRVL mom21 −14.12 and TSM/LRCX/KLAC/MU all red on the 5-day flag near-term profit-taking under the surface.
actions.trigger: Trim into strength / on SOXX losing its rising 20-day; re-add only when rsMom crosses back above 100.
actions.invalidation: SOXX reclaims a rising RS-momentum (>100) with breadth improving — the leadership re-accelerates and the trim was early.

actions.subject: Stay OUT of GLD / SLV / BTC-USD·IBIT (no haven bid)
actions.rationale: Even into a CPI binary there is NO defensive bid in real assets: real-assets-lab scores GLD 15.2 (Downtrend), SLV 0 (washed out, worst on the board), BTC-USD 39.4 / IBIT 31.7 (both Downtrend). Agriculture (DBA 54.8) is the only firm real-asset trend. Do not reach for havens as the CPI hedge — the hedge is cheap index vol, not gold.
actions.structuralAnchor: GLD mom63 −15.96, SLV mom63 −24.6 (vol 76.6), BTC mom63 −21.48 — a risk-on / disinflationary-at-the-margin real-asset tape, not a fear tape.
actions.trigger: Re-engage metals only if GLD reclaims an uptrend AND the SLV model score lifts off 0; re-engage BTC only above its downtrend.
actions.invalidation: A hot CPI sparks a genuine safe-haven gold breakout (GLD reclaims trend) — then the no-haven read is stale and gold becomes the tell.

dataAsOf.bars: Tier-A pre-close refresh 2026-07-13T19:02:25.678Z (263 series), cash session OPEN. SAME distinct market bar as the 7/13 morning run — under the distinct-bar persistence rule Mon 7/13 is ONE observation (2nd distinct obs after Fri 7/10's close), so today's morning→pre-close drift is intraday evidence, NOT a new persistence observation. Intraday drift since the 7/13 morning run (honest, market OPEN): SPY 753→749.78 (−0.43% on the day, still bull-stack 20>50>200, 50-day ~740.6 +1.23%, 200-day ~691.6 +8.42%, −1.03% off the 52-week high ~757.6; mom126/252 +9.32%/+21.14%), QQQ 718.33→712.7 (−0.78%, now −0.41% UNDER its 50-day ~715.6 — the fresh caution flag; mom63 +16.76, mom252 +28.93), SPMO 149.62 (+18.08% over its 200-day), VGT 115.65 (+16.59%), MSFT 389.36→393.19 (still BEAR-stack, −2.37% below its 50-day ~402.7, −10.67% below its 200-day ~440.2, −27.01% off its high ~538.7, but firming intraday mom5 +1.67). The tape de-risked into the close ahead of CPI; all core bull-stacks intact.
dataAsOf.options: Same-origin data/options snapshot refreshed 2026-07-13T19:00:35.728Z (22 symbols: SPX 7521.54, NDX 29286, VIX 17.1 spot, plus single-names). Specific SPY flip / call-wall / put-wall levels and MSFT implied move are NOT surfaced in the Tier-A slice — RE-PULL the chain live in the gamma / options-structure labs before sizing any level trade or the 7/17 OPEX pin. Into the close the actionable read is DIRECTION (hedge the overnight gap), not a precise wall.
dataAsOf.macro: VIX 16.95 Tier-A (options-chain VIX spot 17.1) — UP again on the day (16.44 morning → 16.95), the tape adding a little vol into the CPI print but still BELOW the 3-pt regime-change threshold, so the regime is unchanged: positive-gamma / orderly. Fear&Greed NOT fetched this run (composite reads 0 only because F&G is null — VIX-only, not a neutral tape). VIX term structure (VIX9D/VIX3M) NOT in the Tier-A slice — re-pull live. WTI CARRIED/STALE ~$71.5 (last web-verify; USO Mixed, mom21 −12.4) — softer crude is a softer-headline-CPI tail if it holds into 8:30 tomorrow.
dataAsOf.events: Near-term calendar CARRIED from the config macroEvents (7/11 web verification vs BLS / FRED / Census / U.S. Treasury / CME FedWatch / Kalshi); dates are structural/confirmed, consensus print figures remain ESTIMATES — RE-VERIFY each at release. IMMINENT (T−1): June CPI Tue 7/14 8:30am ET (BLS) COLLIDES with a HEAVY Q2 big-bank open — JPMorgan + Wells Fargo + Bank of America + Citigroup + Goldman Sachs ALL pre-open (~7am ET) the SAME morning (Morgan Stanley 7/15). Then June PPI 7/15 8:30am + MS; June retail sales + weekly jobless claims 7/16 8:30am; monthly OPEX 7/17. FOMC 7/29 2:00pm (non-SEP, no new dots) collides with the MSFT FY26 Q4 print AMC 7/29; July NFP 8/7. FOMC path (CARRIED, CME FedWatch / Kalshi 7/11): ~79% HOLD / ~2% cut / ~15-18% HIKE tail — funds 3.50-3.75% held four straight meetings, no 2026 cuts, hawkish; a hot 7/14 CPI re-arms the hike tail rather than merely delaying cuts.

fearGreed.band: not fetched this run (VIX-only composite — refresh live, do not assume a band)

vix.term: VIX 16.95 (Tier-A), options-chain VIX spot 17.1 — up +0.51 on the day from the 16.44 morning read as the tape adds vol into the CPI print, but still well UNDER the 3-pt regime-change threshold, so this is complacency easing at the margin, NOT a flip. Term structure (VIX9D/VIX3M) NOT in the Tier-A slice — re-pull live. Falsifier: a VIX push >19-20 or IVTS crossing into backwardation (>1.0).

breadth.note: STRUCTURALLY BROAD but leadership breadth is COOLING with NO clean Leading sector this bar. Tool-aligned RRG (2-week RS accel): rotation INTO XLE (Improving ↑, accel +1.13, best on the board) and XLC (Improving ↑, accel +0.98, but bear-stacked −1.92% below its 200-day); rotation OUT / Weakening ↓ for XLK (accel −0.15), XLV (−1.52), XLI (−2.49, sharpest roll) and XLF, the prior lone leader, now Peaking ⚠ (accel −0.25). The structural RS leader remains XLK (3m/6m RS +16.65/+16.69, +18.79% over its 200-day) but it is cooling. Strongest positive acceleration on the board is the MAGS complex (Improving ↑/into, accel +1.53) even though only 1/7 members are bull-stacked. Lagging/neutral: XLY, XLP, XLB, XLRE, XLU. Proxy from sector-member bars, not an exchange breadth feed. Author rotation from RRG state/accel, NOT the raw 1-month RS level.

regime.note: STRUCTURE FIRST, into the 7/13 close: a healthy but EXTENDED tech-led bull near all-time highs, de-risking intraday ahead of a binary. SPY 749.78 bull-stacked (20>50>200), −1.03% off its 52-week high, +8.42% over a rising 200-day, mom126/252 +9.32%/+21.14%. Same distinct bar as the morning run; the intraday drift (SPY −0.43%, QQQ −0.78% and now UNDER its 50-day, VIX +0.51) is de-risking into CPI, not a regime flip (under the 3-pt threshold). Three features on top of the intact bull: (1) a LEADERSHIP shift with NO clean Leading sector — XLK/SOXX Weakening ↓/out despite the board’s best structural RS, XLF flipped to Peaking ⚠ right before the bank open, rotation INTO is early (XLE Improving/tangled; XLC Improving/bear-stacked), MAGS the strongest positive accel (+1.53); (2) the lone STRUCTURAL crack, MSFT (bear-stack, −27.01% off its high) firming intraday (mom5 +1.67) but not reclaiming its 50-day (~403); (3) NO haven bid — GLD 15.2 / SLV 0 / BTC 39.4 all Downtrend, only agriculture (DBA 54.8) firm. All under a HAWKISH-HOLD Fed (7/29 ~79% hold / ~15-18% HIKE, no 2026 cuts). Posture into the bell: HOLD the bull-stacks; HEDGE the overnight CPI gap while VIX is cheap; take XLI→XLE only on an RS-cross; TRIM stretched semis; stay OUT of havens; do NOT call an MSFT bottom until it reclaims ~403.

backdrop.primaryTrend: Extended structural bull near all-time highs, de-risking intraday into the 7/14 CPI + bank-open binary. SPY 749.78 bull-stacked (20>50>200), −1.03% off the 52-week high, +8.42% over a rising 200-day; SPMO/VGT bull-stacked, QQQ bull-stacked but slipping fractionally under its 50-day. Leadership rotates UNDER an intact index: tech/semis cooling on the 2-week accel, no clean Leading sector, XLF peaking into its own earnings, MAGS turning up on thin breadth. The single structural exception is MSFT (bear-stack, −27.01% off high) firming but unrepaired.
backdrop.trendEvidence: SPY mom126 +9.32 / mom252 +21.14, +1.23% over the 50-day (~740.6), +8.42% over the 200-day (~691.6). QQQ mom63 +16.76, mom252 +28.93, but −0.41% UNDER its 50-day (~715.6) after the intraday fade. SPMO +18.08% / VGT +16.59% over their 200-days. Sector breadth broad — 8/11 bull-stacked and above their 200-days. SOXX 12/12 above the 200-day, +43.65% over it (extended). Trend-intact readings; the intraday pullback is shallow and holds structural support.

support: gamma put wall — re-pull live (Tier-A walls not surfaced)

support: 50-day ~715.6 (spot −0.41% — just LOST it intraday, the hold-or-fail line)
QQQ.note: QQQ 712.7 slipped −0.41% under its rising 50-day on the intraday fade — a fresh caution flag, not yet a confirmed break (needs a daily close below). MA levels from Tier-A ma-distance THIS run (7/13 pre-close).

support: in a DOWNTREND — bear-stack; firming intraday (mom5 +1.67) but no base confirmed
MSFT.note: MSFT 393.19 bear-stacked, −27.01% off its 52-week high (~538.7). Firming intraday (mom5 +1.67) but NOT a trend change until it reclaims the 50-day (~403). Idiosyncratic — QQQ/VGT/SPMO remain bull-stacked. The 7/29 capex guide is the tell.

backdrop.macroCycle: HAWKISH-HOLD Fed: funds 3.50-3.75% held four straight meetings, no 2026 cuts priced, hawkish. 7/29 FOMC (non-SEP) ~79% hold / ~2% cut / ~15-18% HIKE tail (CARRIED, CME FedWatch / Kalshi 7/11). A hot 7/14 CPI re-arms the hike tail rather than merely delaying cuts — the asymmetry the short-vol tape is underpricing into the close. WTI carried/STALE ~$71.5; USO Mixed with mom21 −12.4 — softer crude is a softer-headline-CPI tail if it holds.
globalBackdrop: GLOBAL ROTATION is Asia-led and confirms the onshore semis / AI-hardware supply chain: EWS (Singapore) leads at 73.1/100 vs ACWI (rel21 +8.97, Strong uptrend), EWT (Taiwan) 72.9 next (rel63 +24.48, rel126 +46.85), then EWC 58.8, EWJ 56, EWY 55.4, EWU 53.9 — but EVERY leader carries an FX headwind, so the equity strength is local-currency, not a weak-dollar tailwind (deep-link: global-rotation-lab.html).

AI-CAPEX CYCLE is the dominant secular driver — MSFT’s structural breakdown (bear-stack, −27.01% off its high) vs the AI-hardware/semis bull-stacks (VGT/SPMO near highs, SOXX +43.65% over its 200-day, AMD mom63 +118.9, MU mom63 +121) IS the "show-me" (software) vs "build-it" (hardware/semis) dispersion at the single-name level. The MSFT capex guide 7/29 is the tell (deep-link: ai-capex-strategy-lab.html).

REAL ASSETS show NO haven bid even into the CPI binary: GLD Downtrend (15.2, mom63 −15.96), SLV washed out (0, mom63 −24.6, vol 76.6), BTC/IBIT Downtrend (39.4 / 31.7). Agriculture (DBA 54.8) is the only firm real-asset trend; broad commodities (DBC 42.5 / PDBC 42.7) and oil (USO 29.7) are Mixed. Net: risk-on, disinflationary-at-the-margin (deep-link: real-assets-lab.html).
backdrop.pricedIn: A benign-to-in-line CPI and an orderly bank open are the base case the short-vol tape (VIX 16.95) is positioned for — the ~47% "in-line" scenario. What is NOT priced into the close: the overnight-into-CPI gap, a hot print that re-arms the ~15-18% hike tail, and any bank-earnings read-through that confirms/denies the XLF Peaking flip.
backdrop.asymmetry: Skewed to the downside into 7/14: spot is −1.03% off the high with VIX only 16.95 and QQQ just under its 50-day, so a hot CPI or a bad bank open has more room to move price than an in-line print (already largely discounted). Upside case (cool CPI) lets the cooling leaders re-accelerate and fades the hike tail.
backdrop.whatWouldChangeIt: A confirmed SPY close below the 50-day (~741) or QQQ failing to reclaim ~716; VIX pushing >19-20 or the term structure inverting; XLF’s Peaking flip confirmed by a rejected bank-earnings open; or MSFT reclaiming its 50-day (~403) to end the lone structural downtrend. A hot CPI is the single fastest regime-changer given the hawkish-hold hike tail.

attention.structuralAnchor: VIX 16.95 into the densest binary of the month, T−1 into the close
attention.title: June CPI (Tue 7/14 8:30 ET) collides with the heavy big-bank open — hedge before the bell
attention.what: June CPI prints the SAME morning JPM/WFC/BAC/C/GS all report pre-open (~7am ET); consensus figures are ESTIMATES (re-verify at release). The tape de-risked into today’s close (SPY −0.43%, QQQ lost its 50-day, VIX +0.51).
attention.why: This is the marquee catalyst into a short-vol tape near the highs, and it is now T−1 into the close — the last liquid window to hedge cheaply. A hot print re-arms the ~15-18% hike tail; the bank prints then drive the XLF Peaking ⚠ read-through. Position BEFORE, don’t react after.

attention.structuralAnchor: XLE Improving ↑, accel +1.13 (board best); XLI Weakening ↓, accel −2.49 (sharpest roll)
attention.title: Energy is the freshest rotation INTO as industrials roll — trade the XLE RS-cross
attention.what: The 2-week RRG accel puts XLE (Improving ↑, rsMom6m +14.7, +11.65% over its 200-day) as the best-accelerating sector while XLI rolls hardest (accel −2.49) and XLK/SOXX cool. XLE rsRatio 99.71, one cross from the Leading quadrant.
attention.why: A defensible early cyclical rotation that softer crude (a softer-headline-CPI tail) supports. Add XLE on the RS-ratio cross above 100; fund it by trimming the rolling XLI.

attention.title: Semis are the most stretched leader into the print — trim the extension, keep the core
attention.what: SOXX has the strongest structure on the board but is Weakening ↓/out on the 2-week accel (rsMom1m −1.42) and far above trend; MRVL mom21 −14.12 and TSM/LRCX/KLAC/MU all red on the 5-day show near-term profit-taking under the surface.
attention.why: A stretched leader into a binary is where the sharpest air-pocket lives. Trim into strength / on a lost 20-day; re-add when rsMom crosses back above 100. De-risking the extension, not selling the AI-hardware theme.

attention.structuralAnchor: QQQ 712.7 now −0.41% UNDER its 50-day (~715.6); MSFT bear-stack −10.67% below its 200-day
attention.title: QQQ slipped under its 50-day intraday — the hold-or-fail line into CPI
attention.what: The afternoon fade pushed QQQ fractionally below its rising 50-day for the first time this run; it is a caution flag, not yet a confirmed break (needs a daily close below). MSFT (the lone crack) firmed intraday (mom5 +1.67) but has not reclaimed ~403.
attention.why: The 50-day is the structural hold-or-fail line; a CPI-driven close below it would confirm the leadership cooling has reached the index. HOLD the core but watch this level — it defines the hedge’s payoff and the "add vs wait" decision on 7/14.

attention.structuralAnchor: XLF Peaking ⚠ (rotation out, accel −0.25) into its own five-name earnings open
attention.title: XLF flipped to Peaking right before the bank tape opens INTO CPI
attention.what: Financials — the prior lone leader — rolled to Peaking ⚠ (rsMom1m still +3.9 but decelerating) exactly as JPM/WFC/BAC/C/GS report pre-open tomorrow. The bank tape opens straight into the CPI number (NII + credit-cost focus).
attention.why: A Peaking flip that a rejected earnings open would CONFIRM is a high-information event: it tells you whether the last cyclical leader is topping. Watch the bank reaction as the sector-rotation tell, not just the single names.

attention.structuralAnchor: GLD 15.2 / SLV 0 / BTC 39.4 — all Downtrend; DBA 54.8 the only firm trend
attention.title: No haven bid into the CPI binary — the hedge is cheap index vol, not gold
attention.what: real-assets-lab scores gold, silver and bitcoin all in Downtrend even as equities de-risk into the print; agriculture (DBA) is the only real-asset uptrend and the broad basket (DBC 42.5) / oil (USO 29.7) are Mixed.
attention.why: It tells you the tape is a risk-on / disinflationary-at-the-margin regime, not a fear regime — do NOT reach for havens as the CPI hedge. The defined-risk index-vol hedge is the right instrument; a gold breakout would be the signal the regime changed.

attention.structuralAnchor: MAGS Improving ↑, accel +1.53 (board best) but only 1/7 bull-stacked
attention.title: MAGS shows the strongest acceleration on the thinnest breadth — not yet a trend
attention.what: The mega-cap complex has the board’s strongest positive 2-week acceleration (+1.53) carried by AAPL (at its high) and META (mom21 +15.89), but only 1/7 members are bull-stacked and MSFT/TSLA lag — an early-accumulation signal under a weak-breadth surface.
attention.why: A speculative WATCH, not an actionable add — it needs breadth to broaden (>1/7 bull-stacked) to confirm rather than a two-name surge into a CPI binary. Kept sub-gate as an honest watch item.

events.event: June CPI + heavy Q2 big-bank open (JPM/WFC/BAC/C/GS)
events.consensus: ESTIMATE (re-verify at release) — 8:30 ET CPI, headline/core MoM cooling vs prior; banks report ~7am ET, NII + credit costs in focus

scenarios.name: cool
scenarios.expectedEffect: overall: bullish — trend extends, cooling leaders (XLK/SOXX) can re-accelerate, hike tail fades; rates down, long-duration bid

scenarios.name: inline
scenarios.expectedEffect: overall: muted — bull holds, rotation read unchanged; the bank prints then drive the XLF Peaking ⚠ read-through

scenarios.name: hot
scenarios.expectedEffect: overall: bearish — re-arms the ~15-18% hike tail, long-duration de-rate, breaks complacency into OPEX; the tail the short-vol tape (VIX 16.95) is unhedged for

events.psychologyNote: The single fastest regime-changer, now T−1 into the close. A short-vol tape near the highs is positioned for in-line; the overnight gap and the hot-print hike-tail are the unpriced risks — hedge before the bell.

psychology.read: NAMED REGIME: a calm, positive-gamma, complacency-EASING tech-led bull with COOLING leadership and NO clean Leading sector, de-risking INTO a binary. The crowd’s posture is constructive-but-crowded — short-vol (VIX 16.95, only +0.51 up on the day), long the intact bull-stacks, and positioned for an in-line CPI (~47%). Into the close the tape showed the pre-catalyst tell: an all-afternoon fade (SPY −0.43%, QQQ −0.78% and UNDER its 50-day), VIX ticking up, XLF rolling to Peaking ⚠ right before the bank open. This is the classic pre-catalyst setup — the tape is orderly because dealer gamma is positive and no one has been forced to hedge, NOT because the risk is small. What would FALSIFY the read: a VIX push >19-20 or term-structure inversion (regime flip), an SPY close below the 50-day (~741) or QQQ failing to reclaim ~716 (structural break), a hot CPI re-arming the hike tail (the biggest single falsifier), or MAGS broadening + tech re-accelerating (bull re-leads and the cooling was just a pause).
inputs: SAME distinct bar as the 7/13 morning run; intraday drift: SPY −0.43% on the day, QQQ −0.78% (now −0.41% UNDER its 50-day), VIX 16.44→16.95 (+0.51, under the 3-pt threshold) — de-risking into CPI, not a regime flip
RRG rotation: NO clean Leading sector; XLF flipped Leading→Peaking ⚠ (accel −0.25); XLK/SOXX Weakening/out; XLE/XLC Improving/into (early); MAGS strongest positive accel (+1.53)
VIX 16.95, term structure not in the Tier-A slice (re-pull live); Fear&Greed not fetched (VIX-only composite)
Real assets: no haven bid even into the binary — GLD 15.2 / SLV 0 / BTC 39.4 all Downtrend; DBA 54.8 the only firm trend (risk-on, disinflationary-at-the-margin)
Catalyst cluster, T−1: 7/14 CPI + heavy bank open, 7/15 PPI+MS, 7/16 retail+claims, 7/17 OPEX, 7/29 FOMC+MSFT — under a hawkish-hold Fed (~79% hold / ~15-18% hike tail, carried)
psychology.tiltOnProbabilities: The base case remains bullish-to-neutral (in-line CPI ~47%, bull holds), but the intraday fade tilts probability weight toward the DOWNSIDE tail into the close: VIX +0.51, QQQ losing its 50-day, and XLF’s leadership roll all say the complacency is starting to price the catalyst. Net: same structural thesis, higher hedge value into 7/14 (do it before the bell), rotation calls (XLI→XLE) taken only on an RS-cross, and semis trimmed for the extension — not sold.

+4.3% 5-day bounce, tangled stack — the semis swing tell
the lone structural crack; firming intraday, not a reclaim

groups.note: MAGS is Improving ↑/into with the board’s strongest positive acceleration (+1.53) — the mega-cap complex turning UP on the 2-week accel even though only 1/7 members are bull-stacked (breadth 1/7). Leadership is narrow and top-heavy: AAPL at its 52-week high and META’s +15.89 mom21 / +10.13 mom5 surge carry the complex, NVDA is bouncing (+4.3 mom5), while MSFT (the crack) and TSLA (−6.47 mom5) lag. An EARLY-accumulation signal under a weak-breadth surface — WATCH for broadening (>1/7 bull-stacked) to confirm, don’t chase into the CPI binary. Author from the RRG state/accel, not the raw 1-month RS.

groups.note: SOXX is Weakening ↓/out on the 2-week accel (rsMom1m −1.42) but keeps the board’s strongest structure — 8/12 bull-stacked, 12/12 above the 200-day, +43.65% over it. The extension is the risk into a binary: AMD/TXN/AMAT/LRCX/KLAC/MU are all far above trend while MRVL (mom21 −14.12) and TSM/LRCX/KLAC/MU red on the 5-day show near-term profit-taking. TRIM the extension into strength / on a lost 20-day; re-add when rsMom crosses back above 100 — de-risking a stretched leader, not selling the AI-hardware theme. Author from the RRG state/accel, not the raw 1-month RS.

QQQ.status: $712.70 — BULL-STACK (20>50>200) but slipped −0.41% UNDER its 50-day (~715.6) on the intraday fade — the structural hold-or-fail line and this run’s fresh caution flag (needs a daily close below to confirm). +11.85% over the 200-day (~637.2), −4.38% off its 52-week high (~745.3); mom63 +16.76, mom252 +28.93. Tech is COOLING on the 2-week accel (XLK Weakening ↓/out) but that’s decelerating momentum, not a break — HOLD, watch the 50-day reclaim, carry the defined-risk hedge into CPI.

SPMO.status: $149.62 — BULL-STACK, +0.44% over the 50-day, +18.08% over the 200-day; mom63 +21.49, mom252 +34.22. The strongest structural trend in the watchlist and a top-3 risk-adjusted momentum ETF (score 0.3431, behind JMOM). Core long; hold, add only on strength, don’t chase into CPI.

VGT.status: $115.65 — BULL-STACK, +0.25% over the 50-day, +16.59% over the 200-day; mom63 +24.63, mom252 +37.76. A top-3 risk-adjusted momentum ETF (0.3292). Pure-tech core long; hold through the cooling, the bull-stack + RS are intact.

MSFT.status: $393.19 — BEAR-STACK, −2.37% below its 50-day (~402.7), −10.67% below its 200-day (~440.2), −27.01% off its 52-week high (~538.7); mom5 +1.67 (firming intraday), mom21 −1.05, mom63 +6.25. A mild stabilization inside a downtrend — NOT a trend change until it reclaims the 50-day (~403). The lone structural crack while QQQ/VGT/SPMO stay bull-stacked; the 7/29 capex guide is the tell for the AI "show-me vs build-it" dispersion.

experimental.title: Pre-catalyst de-risk detector (intraday-fade + VIX-uptick + leader-flip)
experimental.note: Method: on the session BEFORE a Tier-1 macro binary (CPI/PPI/FOMC/NFP), score a composite "de-risk tell" = sign(intraday index drift) + sign(VIX day-change) + count of sectors flipping Leading→Peaking on the fresh bar (this run: SPY −0.43%, VIX +0.51, XLF Leading→Peaking = 3/3 de-risk). Then event-study the forward CPI-day gap and 1-/3-day return conditioned on the composite, to test whether a pre-print de-risk predicts a smaller adverse gap (crowd already hedged) or a larger one (crowd still offside). Inputs: intraday open→pre-close index drift, VIX day-change, per-sector RRG state transitions, the dated catalyst. No owning tool captures the pre-catalyst de-risk state-transition timing today.

experimental.title: Rotation-flip detector (Leading→Peaking)
experimental.note: Method: flag any sector whose RRG state crosses from Leading to Peaking with negative acceleration on a fresh bar (this run: XLF accel −0.25), then event-study the forward 5/10-day sector return conditioned on whether the coincident earnings/catalyst confirmed or denied the roll (here: the 7/14 bank open confirms/denies the XLF flip). Inputs: per-sector rsRatio/rsMom/accel history + the sector’s next scheduled catalyst. No owning tool captures the state-transition timing today.
